cross_reactive: apple, cherry, peach, pear, hazelnut, carrot, celery, kiwi
action: Oral allergy syndrome counseling. Cooking denatures PR-10, reducing risk.
Monitor soy consumption in birch-pollen-allergic patients. Fermented soy safer.
OAS counseling. Typically mild oral symptoms. Cooking reduces risk.
Celery-mugwort-spice syndrome counseling. LTP-mediated reactions may be severe.
Avoid herbal teas with chamomile. Monitor sunflower seed consumption.
OAS counseling. Usually mild. Cooking eliminates profilin reactivity.
Monitor tomato consumption during pollen season.
Lupin flour avoidance counseling. Common in EU bakery products.
Individual nut assessment recommended. Blanket avoidance may be unnecessary.
Individual assessment. Tropomyosin-driven; also cross-reacts with dust mite.
Monitor dust mite/cockroach exposure in shellfish-allergic patients.
Avoid all ruminant milks. Plant-based alternatives recommended.
If allergic to one, test for the other before consumption.
If allergic to one, test for the other before consumption.
Current guidelines: safe to administer. No special precautions for mild egg allergy.

rate: Variable (IgE wheat allergy differs from celiac disease)
action: Distinguish IgE-mediated wheat allergy from celiac disease. Different management.

cross_reactive: mango, passion fruit, papaya, fig, pineapple
action: Extended latex-fruit counseling beyond classic banana/avocado/kiwi/chestnut.
Test for both venoms. VIT may cover both with wasp venom.
Component-resolved diagnosis recommended to distinguish true vs. CCD cross-reactivity.
Component-resolved diagnosis recommended.
Some tolerate specific fish. Individual assessment by species via component testing.
Individual legume assessment. Most soy-allergic patients tolerate other legumes.
Skin testing for both before mRNA vaccines or PEG-containing drugs.

cross_reactive: MMR vaccine, varicella vaccine, influenza vaccine (gelatin-containing)
action: Gelatin skin test before gelatin-containing vaccines if prior reaction.
COX-2 selective inhibitors (celecoxib) generally tolerated. Aspirin desensitization for cardiac indications.
